nameMeanings.name: Beauple
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Colette, Laurier, Treffle, Adelard, Alphonse, Dany, Fernand, Germaine, Gilles, Henri.

French (Beaupré): habitational name from (Le) Beaupré or (Le) Beau Pré, names of several places in various parts of France, or a topographic name from beau pré ‘beautiful meadow’, apparently related to the site of an estate or property.

nameMeanings.name: Reginald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Norman origin, derived from Reginaldus, a Latinized form of Reynold influenced by Latin regina ‘queen’. The full form is now regarded as very formal, and bearers generally shorten it to Reg in ordinary usage.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: Reginald II Beauple was born in 1160, in South Molton, Devon, England, United Kingdom as the son of Richard Beauple and *E MRS Richard BEAUPEL. He had at least 1 son with Mrs Reginald BEAUPLE II.
